betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Network Planes§f:
The §6Network Planes §fare §ecable parts§f that transfer items between the ME network and §eadjacent block inventories§f. They are the simplest and most energy-efficient way to do §6item I/O§f without a full §6ME Interface§f.

§6Plane Family§f:
- §bME Annihilation Plane §f(appliedenergistics2:part:300) — pulls items from the adjacent block into the network
- §bME Identity Annihilation Plane §f(appliedenergistics2:part:301) — same as Annihilation Plane, but preserves the §bitem's NBT§f
- §bME Formation Plane §f(appliedenergistics2:part:302) — places items from the network into the adjacent block
- §bME Fluid Annihilation Plane §f(appliedenergistics2:part:302) — pulls fluids
- §bME Fluid Formation Plane §f(appliedenergistics2:part:302) — pushes fluids
- §bME Inverted Plane §f(variants) — can be §6inverted§f with an §6Inverter Card§f to flip push/pull direction

§6Production Path (network/parts/planes_annihilation_alt.json)§f:
The §6ME Annihilation Plane §fpattern is "aaa / bcb":
- §ba§f: §6Fluix Crystal§f (3, appliedenergistics2:material:7 or any crystal in the #appliedenergistics2:fluixCrystal tag)
- §bb§f: §6Iron Ingot§f (1, ingotIron)
- §cc§f: §6Annihilation Core§f (appliedenergistics2:material:44)
- Yields 1× ME Annihilation Plane

The §6ME Formation Plane §f(network/parts/planes_formation_alt.json) is identical but uses a §6Formation Core §fand a §6Pure Fluix Crystal§f.

§6Function (PartAnnihilationPlane.java:88, PartFormationPlane.java:70)§f:
- §bAnnihilation Plane§f: each tick, breaks the block in front of it and stores the drops in the network
- §bFormation Plane§f: each tick, places configured items into the world in front of it
- §bIdentity Annihilation Plane§f: preserves §bblock entity data§f — needed for §6chests with items§f, §6machines with stored energy§f, etc.
- §bTick rate§f: the planes operate at §b1 block per 8 ticks§f by default, configurable via §6Acceleration Cards§f
- §bPower cost§f: roughly §b1 AE/t per plane§f at idle; scales with operation speed

§6Direct Descendants (TaskRetrieval graph)§f:
The jei-graph shows 3 items the Annihilation Plane is a component of:
- §6ME Identity Annihilation Plane§f (network/parts/planes_annihilation_fluid.json variant)
- §6Crafting patterns §fthat reference plane behaviour
- §6ME Annihilation Plane §f(fluid variant, appliedenergistics2:part:302)

§6Practical Use§f:
- §eTip§f: place a §6ME Annihilation Plane §ffacing a §6chest§f, then a §6ME Storage Bus §f on the same chest — items dropped in the chest go directly to the network
- §eTip§f: pair a §6Formation Plane §fwith a §6ME Pattern Terminal §f and §6Crafting CPU §f to auto-place crafted blocks (e.g. cobblestone generators)
- §cWarning§f: a Formation Plane will not place §6bedrock§f, §6end portal frames§f, or blocks with §bblock entities§f unless using a §6Identity Formation Plane §f
- §cWarning§f: an Annihilation Plane requires redstone power to be disabled; if you see it pulsing off, attach a §6Level Emitter §f or a §6Toggle Bus §f
